mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as the market prices in stronger economic activity.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, the correlation between RSAFS 3-month growth and forward 20-day SPX return is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) or the Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the federal funds rate to near zero signals the end of an easing cycle or a crisis response, compressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations; this threshold trigger historically precedes strong positive returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS drops to 0.25% or below, average 60-day SPX return is not positive or is not significantly different from unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10) over at least 5 such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth is high and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low and rising, the labor market tightness signals late-cycle dynamics; the joint behavior of rising employment and falling unemployment (or vice versa) predicts negative equity returns as wage pressures squeeze margins.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS change * UNRATE change) does not significantly predict 20-day SPX returns in a multivariate regression (p > 0.05) or out-of-sample Rank IC < 0.03 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining sharply signals a weakening economy that depresses corporate earnings expectations; this effect is especially strong when the housing sector previously expanded rapidly, as it indicates a cyclical turning point.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 12-month high
failure_condition: In months when HOUST drops >10% from 12-month high, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is not negative on average or is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over at least 10 instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above trend forces the Fed to tighten monetary policy, increasing discount rates and reducing the present value of future earnings, which depresses equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When CPI 12-month inflation exceeds 4%, the average 60-day SPX return is not negative or is not significantly different from non-inflationary periods (permutation test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growing strongly while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains accommodative signals a 'goldilocks' environment of strong output without policy drag; this combined condition supports positive equity returns over the coming month.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the 20-day SPX return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the signal's Sharpe ratio is below 0.5 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth accelerations signal expanding aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenues and earnings; this trend is a positive driver for equity returns over the subsequent quarter as macroeconomic momentum persists.
failure_condition: The quarterly GDP growth rate does not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with forward 60-day SPX returns (p > 0.10) or the rank IC is below 0.03 over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) after a period of low levels signals a labor market deterioration that erodes consumer spending and profits; this is particularly damaging when job creation (PAYEMS) simultaneously decelerates, leading to negative equity returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.2% or more in a single month while PAYEMS growth is negative
failure_condition: In weeks triggered by this condition, the average 5-day SPX return is not negative or is not significantly lower than non-triggered weeks (t-test p > 0.10) over at least 30 triggered events.
